title: Nerisyrenia linearifolia
intro: Nerisyrenia linearifolia, commonly known as Eastern American bugleweed or linear-leaved bugleweed, is a perennial plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce anxiety due to its calming effects. Bioactive constituents of Nerisyrenia linearifolia include alkaloids, such as bugleweed alkaloids, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nerisyrenia linearifolia is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat conditions such as hyperthyroidism and hypertension.
taxonomy: Nerisyrenia linearifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is also known as linearleaf moonwort. The plant is native to the southeastern United States. It is often found growing in woodland environments. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as arugula and mustard.
